findings.problem: Sanitization drift at the chapter's hardest moral beat: the affair lands as euphemistic summary ("I slept with someone"), June's immediate impact is abstracted ("it was an update"), and the scene pivots quickly into procedural boundary terms. The draft flinches from embodied betrayal consequence.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rebuild the confession/reaction hinge at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:189, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:191, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:219, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:237 so Eli names concrete affair context and June's response is rendered physically and verbally before checklist negotiation resumes at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:241.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if revised lines around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:189-237 include (a) explicit affair context beyond the euphemism, (b) at least two concrete embodied reaction beats from June, and (c) at least three consecutive consequence-focused paragraphs before boundary logistics begin; otherwise FAIL.

findings.problem: The back third resolves pressure too cleanly in one day: every key relation acknowledges compliance, and the ending lands on schedule confirmation. Consequence remains present conceptually, but dramatic uncertainty drops below chapter-closing tension.
findings.rewrite_direction: Inject one active unresolved cost or setback into the final movement at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:347, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:411, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:469 so the chapter closes with earned instability rather than smooth operational confirmation.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised final sequence (from snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:347 onward) contains at least one unresolved relational or financial threat that remains open at the final paragraph, and the ending line does not function as pure timetable closure; otherwise FAIL.

findings.problem: Boundary negotiation drifts into shared policy cadence: June and Eli both speak in near-contract clauses and mirrored confirmation beats, which blurs idiolect separation and makes high-pressure dialogue sound administratively over-formal.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:241-262, keep the same terms but rewrite delivery so June retains her quick, ironic college-age rhythm and Eli keeps his shame-weighted self-interruptions; reduce mirrored 'term -> acknowledgment' patterning at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:275.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised exchange at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:241-275 preserves all boundary terms while showing clear voice separation: June has at least three lines with colloquial pressure rhythm, Eli has at least two lines with broken/self-revising syntax, and there is no run of three consecutive conditional-policy lines; otherwise FAIL.

findings.problem: Recurring gesture formula: nodding is used as the default emotional beat well beyond the 'more than twice' threshold, flattening distinct moments of concession, shame, and relief into one repeated physical signal.
findings.rewrite_direction: Replace nod-based beats at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:189, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:255, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:277, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:371 with distinct actions tied to each moment's emotional vector (deflection, agreement under pressure, procedural verification, guarded acceptance).
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised chapter keeps no more than two total uses of 'nod'/'nodded' in the cited cluster and each replaced beat is legible as a different emotional state by direct reading; otherwise FAIL.

findings.problem: Interiority lapse: this sequence runs 11 consecutive exterior-only paragraphs (action/dialogue) with no Eli thought, sensation, or self-assessment, shifting the narration into screenplay mode during a transition that should still carry emotional residue.
findings.rewrite_direction: Thread brief close-third interior beats into snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:301-321 (for example at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:311 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:317) so interruption/comedy registers against Eli's current shame and vigilance.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if revised lines 301-321 include at least two separate interiority beats (thought/sensation/self-assessment) and no exterior-only run exceeds nine consecutive paragraphs in that stretch; otherwise FAIL.

findings.problem: Mechanical reuse of style-bible stress vocabulary (terms, transparent, operational, numbers/checklist framing) starts to flatten prose and dialogue into one managerial register, reducing lexical surprise and emotional grain.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework diction at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:253, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:353, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:383, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:413 to keep accountability semantics but vary the language with scene-specific sensory and character-specific phrasing rather than repeated policy tokens.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if revised cited lines retain the same factual commitments while reducing managerial-token repetition to no more than two occurrences across the cluster and adding at least two concrete non-bureaucratic images tied to body/place; otherwise FAIL.

summary: Voice profiles and contraction usage are mostly aligned with the style bible, and canonical continuity beats (apartment move, weekly call boundaries, warehouse-income reality) are intact. The chapter still fails on Booker/craft/prose rigor due to sanitized betrayal rendering, late-stage tension deflation into smooth compliance, dialogue cadence convergence in the boundary block, and repeated prose tics (nodding default, managerial lexicon, and a 10+ paragraph interiority gap).
